desires: Avoid the immediate stress of starting the scholarship application by continuing to ignore the deadline until it's absolutely unavoidable.
Seek a quick dopamine hit from the casual game stream to compensate for my zero energy and rising anxiety without expending effort on social interaction.

goals.description: Continue the casual game stream immediately; do not stop until I feel a distinct drop in anxiety or my energy hits absolute zero.
goals.rationale: My 'impulsive' axis demands instant relief from this drained state, and my 'avoidant' nature makes the friction of starting the scholarship essay unbearable right now. The deadline is 3 days away (short-term horizon), so ignoring it feels rational for immediate survival.

goals.description: Keep the scholarship application tab open but strictly avoid typing; stare at the cursor to maintain the superficial illusion of 'working' without engaging the task.
goals.rationale: I need to satisfy my internal 'achievement' metric just enough to avoid total self-loathing, but my low assertiveness prevents me from actually pushing through the difficulty. This satisfies the 'academic' value priority minimally while protecting my social/mental energy.

goals.description: Silently ignore all incoming messages from teammates and hallmates regarding my absence; no replies until the gaming session concludes.
goals.rationale: Social interaction is high-friction and drains the little energy I have left. My 'avoidant' conflict style dictates that isolation is the safest path to prevent awkwardness or having to explain my procrastination.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances immediate anxiety relief (Goal 1) by satisfying the impulsive need for escape. I'm ignoring teammates (Goal 3) to avoid social friction, and staring at the cursor (Goal 2) is too draining when my energy is already zero.
